element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Rhythm-based aerial tricks, learning controls in enclosed vertical space
Timing and precision with simple mechanics
Wave timing, animation-based feedback, environmental responsiveness
Speed management, navigation with inertia mechanics
Trick execution under speed, terrain interaction

typical_tension_triggers: Competing against time or distance targets
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Episodic stress-relief cycles; each sport is discrete and allows player-driven pacing through menu selection; multiplayer competitive moments provide intensity spikes

spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Developer easter egg: Player name 'PAUL' or 'RACHEL' triggers hidden message 'PP & RME' on roller skating course end

difficulty_curve.early_game: Tutorial-like accessibility with footbag and half-pipe providing low-skill-floor introduction
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Surfing and roller skating introduce environmental responsiveness and inertia management
difficulty_curve.late_game: BMX and flying disc demand higher technical execution and precision timing
notable_spikes: Skateboarding event noted as frustrating by reviewers (control difficulty spike)
Surfing animation-dependent mechanics create skill ceiling

notable_level_patterns.description: Game structured as 6 independent sports events (half-pipe, footbag, surfing, roller skating, BMX, flying disc) selectable from menu; no sequential progression enforced
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Half Pipe (vertical space, aerial trick execution)

notable_level_patterns.description: Surfing event uses outstanding animation and realistic graphics as primary feedback mechanism for player success; animation quality informs mastery context
Games series defined by competitive multiplayer modes with level of control praised across platforms; individual events support head-to-head play
